A test case to verify that false is not strictly equal to true.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks a basic logical condition. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the expression `false === true` evaluates to `false`. The assertion ensures that the condition is logically correct, and if it fails, it will typically throw an error or halt execution, depending on the environment. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic sanity check or a placeholder for more complex logic in a testing framework.
